Imagine fleeing your home with only minutes to pack everything you own. Imagine arriving in a new country where you don't speak the language, where winter feels like death, where you work two jobs and still feel like a nobody. This is the story of a refugee who hated her new country—until one night in a refugee camp changed everything. In the middle of a sandstorm, in 50-degree heat, with no water and no electricity, she found herself wishing for the one thing she hated most: winter. That moment would transform her entire journey.
